title: Epiphyllum cartagense
taxonomy: Epiphyllum cartagense is a species of flowering plant in the family Cactaceae. It is native to South America and belongs to the genus Epiphyllum, which includes other species of epiphytic cacti and succulents. This species is a type of epiphyte, growing on other plants without deriving nutrients from them. Epiphyllum cartagense is a rare and lesser-known species, but it is valued for its unique characteristics and growth habits. Its classification reflects its unique position as a tropical epiphytic cactus.

intro: Epiphyllum cartagense is a species of epiphytic cactus native to the Americas, characterized by its flat, fleshy stems that grow on other plants or surfaces. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Mexico, to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Epiphyllum cartagense include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which have been reported to exhibit antioxidant, antimicrobial, and antiproliferative activities. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as ointments and salves.
